text: 6. Discrete Mathematics
html: 6. Discrete Mathematics
zh: 6. 离散数学

text: 6.1. Finsets and Fintypes
html: 6.1. Finsets and Fintypes
zh: 6.1. 有限集与有限类型

text: 6.2. Counting Arguments
html: 6.2. Counting Arguments
zh: 6.2. 计数论证

text: 6.3. Inductively Defined Types
html: 6.3. Inductively Defined Types
zh: 6.3. 归纳定义的类型

text: Discrete Mathematics is the study of finite sets, objects, and structures. We can count the elements of a finite set, and we can compute finite sums or products over its elements, we can compute maximums and minimums, and so on. We can also study objects that are generated by finitely many applications of certain generating functions, we can define functions by structural recursion, and prove theorems by structural induction. This chapter describes parts of Mathlib that support these activities.
html: Discrete Mathematics is the study of finite sets, objects, and structures. We can count the elements of a finite set, and we can compute finite sums or products over its elements, we can compute maximums and minimums, and so on. We can also study objects that are generated by finitely many applications of certain generating functions, we can define functions by structural recursion, and prove theorems by structural induction. This chapter describes parts of Mathlib that support these activities.
zh: Discrete Mathematics研究有限集合、对象与结构。我们可以计数有限集合的元素，可以计算其元素上的有限和或有限积，可以计算最大值与最小值，等等。我们还可以研究由某些生成函数经有限次应用而生成的对象，可以用结构递归定义函数，并用结构归纳证明定理。本章介绍 Mathlib 中支持这些工作的若干部分。

text: Dealing with finite sets and types in Mathlib can be confusing, because the library offers multiple ways of handling them. In this section we will discuss the most common ones.
html: Dealing with finite sets and types in Mathlib can be confusing, because the library offers multiple ways of handling them. In this section we will discuss the most common ones.
zh: 在 Mathlib 中处理有限集合与有限类型可能令人困惑，因为库提供了多种处理方式。本节将讨论其中最常用的几种。

text: We have already come across the type Finset in Section 5.2 and Section 5.3 . As the name suggests, an element of type Finset α is a finite set of elements of type α . We will call these “finsets.” The Finset data type is designed to have a computational interpretation, and many basic operations on Finset α assume that α has decidable equality, which guarantees that there is an algorithm for testing whether a : α is an element of a finset s .
html: We have already come across the type Finset in Section 5.2 and Section 5.3. As the name suggests, an element of type Finset α is a finite set of elements of type α. We will call these “finsets.” The Finset data type is designed to have a computational interpretation, and many basic operations on Finset α assume that α has decidable equality, which guarantees that there is an algorithm for testing whether a : α is an element of a finset s.
zh: 我们已经在Section 5.2和Section 5.3中见过类型 Finset。顾名思义，类型 Finset α 的一个元素就是由类型 α 的元素构成的有限集。我们称这类对象为“有限集”（finset）。数据类型 Finset 被设计为具有计算解释；Finset α 上许多基本操作都假定 α 具有可判定相等性，这保证存在一种算法可检验 a : α 是否为有限集 s 的元素。

text: If you remove the declaration [DecidableEq α] , Lean will complain on the line #check s ∩ t because it cannot compute the intersection. All of the data types that you should expect to be able to compute with have decidable equality, however, and if you work classically by opening the Classical namespace and declaring noncomputable section , you can reason about finsets of elements of any type at all.
html: If you remove the declaration [DecidableEq α], Lean will complain on the line #check s ∩ t because it cannot compute the intersection. All of the data types that you should expect to be able to compute with have decidable equality, however, and if you work classically by opening the Classical namespace and declaring noncomputable section, you can reason about finsets of elements of any type at all.
zh: 如果移除声明 [DecidableEq α]，Lean 会在 #check s ∩ t 这一行报错，因为它无法计算交。尽管如此，所有你期望能够进行计算的数据类型都有可判定相等性；而若通过打开 Classical 命名空间并声明 noncomputable section 来以经典方式工作，就可以对任意类型的元素所成有限集进行推理。

text: Finsets support most of the set-theoretic operations that sets do:
html: Finsets support most of the set-theoretic operations that sets do:
zh: 有限集支持集合所支持的大多数集合论操作：

text: Note that we have opened the Finset namespace, where theorems specific to finsets are found. If you step through the last example below, you will see applying ext followed by simp reduces the identity to a problem in propositional logic. As an exercise, you can try proving some of set identities from Chapter 4 , transported to finsets.
html: Note that we have opened the Finset namespace, where theorems specific to finsets are found. If you step through the last example below, you will see applying ext followed by simp reduces the identity to a problem in propositional logic. As an exercise, you can try proving some of set identities from Chapter 4, transported to finsets.
zh: 注意，我们已经打开了 Finset 命名空间，其中包含有限集专用的定理。如果逐步查看下面最后一个例子，会看到先应用 ext 再应用 simp 会把该恒等式化归为命题逻辑问题。作为练习，你可以尝试把Chapter 4中的一些集合恒等式迁移到有限集上来证明。

text: You have already seen the notation Finset.range n for the finite set of natural numbers \(\{ 0, 1, \ldots, n-1 \}\) . Finset also allows you to define finite sets by enumerating the elements:
html: You have already seen the notation Finset.range n for the finite set of natural numbers \(\{ 0, 1, \ldots, n-1 \}\). Finset also allows you to define finite sets by enumerating the elements:
zh: 你已经见过记号 Finset.range n，它表示自然数的有限集 \(\{ 0, 1, \ldots, n-1 \}\)。Finset 还允许通过列举元素来定义有限集：

text: There are various ways to get Lean to recognize that order of elements and duplicates do not matter in a set presented in this way.
html: There are various ways to get Lean to recognize that order of elements and duplicates do not matter in a set presented in this way.
zh: 有多种方法可以让 Lean 识别：以这种方式给出的集合中，元素的顺序和重复出现并不重要。

text: You can use insert to add a single element to a Finset, and Finset.erase to delete a single element. Note that erase is in the Finset namespace, but insert is in the root namespace.
html: You can use insert to add a single element to a Finset, and Finset.erase to delete a single element. Note that erase is in the Finset namespace, but insert is in the root namespace.
zh: 可以使用 insert 向一个有限集中加入单个元素，也可以使用 Finset.erase 删除单个元素。注意，erase 位于 Finset 命名空间中，而 insert 位于根命名空间中。

text: In fact, {0, 1, 2} is just notation for insert 0 (insert 1 (singleton 2)) .
html: In fact, {0, 1, 2} is just notation for insert 0 (insert 1 (singleton 2)).
zh: 事实上，{0, 1, 2} 只是 insert 0 (insert 1 (singleton 2)) 的记号。

text: Given a finset s and a predicate P , we can use set-builder notation {x ∈ s | P x} to define the set of elements of s that satisfy P . This is notation for Finset.filter P s , which can also be written s.filter P .
html: Given a finset s and a predicate P, we can use set-builder notation {x ∈ s | P x} to define the set of elements of s that satisfy P. This is notation for Finset.filter P s, which can also be written s.filter P.
zh: 给定有限集 s 和谓词 P，可以用集合构造记号 {x ∈ s | P x} 定义 s 中满足 P 的元素所成集合。这是 Finset.filter P s 的记号，也可以写作 s.filter P。

text: Mathlib knows that the image of a finset under a function is a finset.
html: Mathlib knows that the image of a finset under a function is a finset.
zh: Mathlib 知道，有限集在函数下的像仍然是有限集。

text: Lean also knows that the cartesian product s ×ˢ t of two finsets is a finset, and that the powerset of a finset is a finset. (Note that the notation s ×ˢ t also works for sets.)
html: Lean also knows that the cartesian product s ×ˢ t of two finsets is a finset, and that the powerset of a finset is a finset. (Note that the notation s ×ˢ t also works for sets.)
zh: Lean 也知道两个有限集的笛卡儿积 s ×ˢ t 是有限集，并且有限集的幂集也是有限集。（注意，记号 s ×ˢ t 对集合同样适用。）

text: Defining operations on finsets in terms of their elements is tricky, because any such definition has to be independent of the order in which the elements are presented. Of course, you can always define functions by composing existing operations. Another thing you can do is use Finset.fold to fold a binary operation over the elements, provided that the operation is associative and commutative, since these properties guarantee that the result is independent of the order that the operation is applied. Finite sums, products, and unions are defined in that way. In the last example below, biUnion stands for “bounded indexed union.” With conventional mathematical notation, the expression would be written \(\bigcup_{i ∈ s} g(i)\) .
html: Defining operations on finsets in terms of their elements is tricky, because any such definition has to be independent of the order in which the elements are presented. Of course, you can always define functions by composing existing operations. Another thing you can do is use Finset.fold to fold a binary operation over the elements, provided that the operation is associative and commutative, since these properties guarantee that the result is independent of the order that the operation is applied. Finite sums, products, and unions are defined in that way. In the last example below, biUnion stands for “bounded indexed union.” With conventional mathematical notation, the expression would be written \(\bigcup_{i ∈ s} g(i)\).
zh: 按有限集的元素来定义操作是微妙的，因为任何这样的定义都必须与元素给出的顺序无关。当然，总可以通过复合已有操作来定义函数。另一种做法是使用 Finset.fold 将一个二元运算折叠到各个元素上，前提是该运算满足结合律和交换律；这些性质保证结果与应用运算的顺序无关。有限和、有限积以及有限并正是以这种方式定义的。在下面最后一个例子中，biUnion 表示“有界指标并”。用通常的数学记号，该表达式可写为 \(\bigcup_{i ∈ s} g(i)\)。

text: There is a natural principle of induction on finsets: to prove that every finset has a property, show that the empty set has the property and that the property is preserved when we add one new element to a finset. (The @ symbol in @insert is needed in the induction step of the next example to give names to the parameters a and s because they have been marked implicit. )
html: There is a natural principle of induction on finsets: to prove that every finset has a property, show that the empty set has the property and that the property is preserved when we add one new element to a finset. (The @ symbol in @insert is needed in the induction step of the next example to give names to the parameters a and s because they have been marked implicit. )
zh: 有限集上有一个自然的归纳原则：要证明每个有限集都具有某性质，只需证明空集具有该性质，并证明当向一个有限集加入一个新元素时该性质得以保持。（下一个例子的归纳步骤中需要在 @insert 里使用符号 @，以便给参数 a 和 s 命名，因为它们已被标记为隐式参数。）

text: If s is a finset, Finset.Nonempty s is defined to be ∃ x, x ∈ s . You can use classical choice to pick an element of a nonempty finset. Similarly, the library defines Finset.toList s which uses choice to pick the elements of s in some order.
html: If s is a finset, Finset.Nonempty s is defined to be ∃ x, x ∈ s. You can use classical choice to pick an element of a nonempty finset. Similarly, the library defines Finset.toList s which uses choice to pick the elements of s in some order.
zh: 若 s 是有限集，则 Finset.Nonempty s 被定义为 ∃ x, x ∈ s。可以使用经典选择从非空有限集中选取一个元素。类似地，库定义了 Finset.toList s，它使用选择以某种顺序选取 s 的元素。

text: You can use Finset.min and Finset.max to choose the minimum or maximum element of a finset of elements of a linear order, and similarly you can use Finset.inf and Finset.sup with finsets of elements of a lattice, but there is a catch. What should the minimum element of an empty finset be? You can check that the primed versions of the functions below add a precondition that the finset is nonempty. The non-primed versions Finset.min and Finset.max add a top or bottom element, respectively, to the output type, to handle the case where the finset is empty. The non-primed versions Finset.inf and Finset.sup assume that the lattice comes equipped with a top or bottom element, respectively.
html: You can use Finset.min and Finset.max to choose the minimum or maximum element of a finset of elements of a linear order, and similarly you can use Finset.inf and Finset.sup with finsets of elements of a lattice, but there is a catch. What should the minimum element of an empty finset be? You can check that the primed versions of the functions below add a precondition that the finset is nonempty. The non-primed versions Finset.min and Finset.max add a top or bottom element, respectively, to the output type, to handle the case where the finset is empty. The non-primed versions Finset.inf and Finset.sup assume that the lattice comes equipped with a top or bottom element, respectively.
zh: 可以使用 Finset.min 和 Finset.max 从线性序中元素构成的有限集中选取最小或最大元素；类似地，也可以对格中元素构成的有限集使用 Finset.inf 和 Finset.sup。但这里有一个细节：空有限集的最小元素应当是什么？你可以检查下面这些函数的带撇号版本，它们增加了有限集非空这一前提。无撇号版本 Finset.min 和 Finset.max 分别向输出类型加入一个顶元素或底元素，用来处理有限集为空的情形。无撇号版本 Finset.inf 和 Finset.sup 则分别假定该格配备了顶元素或底元素。

text: Every finset s has a finite cardinality, Finset.card s , which can be written #s when the Finset namespace is open.
html: Every finset s has a finite cardinality, Finset.card s, which can be written #s when the Finset namespace is open.
zh: 每个有限集 s 都有一个有限基数 Finset.card s；当 Finset 命名空间打开时，它可写作 #s。

text: The next section is all about reasoning about cardinality.
html: The next section is all about reasoning about cardinality.
zh: 下一节将专门讨论关于基数的推理。

text: When formalizing mathematics, one often has to make a decision as to whether to express one’s definitions and theorems in terms of sets or types. Using types often simplifies notation and proofs, but working with subsets of a type can be more flexible. The type-based analogue of a finset is a fintype , that is, a type Fintype α for some α . By definition, a fintype is just a data type that comes equipped with a finset univ that contains all its elements.
html: When formalizing mathematics, one often has to make a decision as to whether to express one’s definitions and theorems in terms of sets or types. Using types often simplifies notation and proofs, but working with subsets of a type can be more flexible. The type-based analogue of a finset is a fintype, that is, a type Fintype α for some α. By definition, a fintype is just a data type that comes equipped with a finset univ that contains all its elements.
zh: 在形式化数学时，常常必须决定是用集合还是用类型来表述定义和定理。使用类型往往能简化记号和证明，但使用某个类型的子集则可能更灵活。有限集的类型化对应物是fintype，即某个 α 上的类型 Fintype α。按照定义，有限类型只是一个配备了有限集 univ 的数据类型，而该有限集包含其全部元素。

text: Fintype.card α is equal to the cardinality of the corresponding finset.
html: Fintype.card α is equal to the cardinality of the corresponding finset.
zh: Fintype.card α 等于相应有限集的基数。

text: We have already seen a prototypical example of a fintype, namely, the types Fin n for each n . Lean recognizes that the fintypes are closed under operations like the product operation.
html: We have already seen a prototypical example of a fintype, namely, the types Fin n for each n. Lean recognizes that the fintypes are closed under operations like the product operation.
zh: 我们已经见过有限类型的一个典型例子，即对每个 n 给出的类型 Fin n。Lean 能够识别有限类型在诸如积这样的操作下封闭。

text: Any element s of Finset α can be coerced to a type (↑s : Type α) , namely, the subtype of elements of α that are contained in s . Moreover, Lean knows that ↑s is a fintype.
html: Any element s of Finset α can be coerced to a type (↑s : Type α), namely, the subtype of elements of α that are contained in s. Moreover, Lean knows that ↑s is a fintype.
zh: Finset α 的任意元素 s 都可以强制转换为一个类型 (↑s : Type α)，即 α 中属于 s 的元素所成的子类型。此外，Lean 知道 ↑s 是有限类型。

text: Lean and Mathlib use type class inference to track the additional structure on fintypes, namely, the universal finset that contains all the elements. In other words, you can think of a fintype as an algebraic structure equipped with that extra data. Chapter 7 explains how this works.
html: Lean and Mathlib use type class inference to track the additional structure on fintypes, namely, the universal finset that contains all the elements. In other words, you can think of a fintype as an algebraic structure equipped with that extra data. Chapter 7 explains how this works.
zh: Lean 和 Mathlib 使用type class inference来跟踪有限类型上的额外结构，也就是包含所有元素的全体有限集。换言之，可以把有限类型看作配备了这项额外数据的代数结构。Chapter 7将说明这一机制如何工作。

text: The art of counting things is a central part of combinatorics. Mathlib contains several basic identities for counting elements of finsets:
html: The art of counting things is a central part of combinatorics. Mathlib contains several basic identities for counting elements of finsets:
zh: 计数的技巧是组合数学的核心组成部分。Mathlib 含有若干用于计数有限集元素的基本恒等式：

text: Opening the Finset namespace allows us to use the notation #s for s.card , as well as to use the shortened names card_union and so on.
html: Opening the Finset namespace allows us to use the notation #s for s.card, as well as to use the shortened names card_union and so on.
zh: 打开 Finset 命名空间后，可以使用记号 #s 表示 s.card，也可以使用 card_union 等缩短后的名称。

text: Mathlib can also count elements of fintypes:
html: Mathlib can also count elements of fintypes:
zh: Mathlib 也可以计数有限类型的元素：

text: When the Fintype namespace is not open, we have to use Fintype.card instead of card .
html: When the Fintype namespace is not open, we have to use Fintype.card instead of card.
zh: 当 Fintype 命名空间未打开时，必须使用 Fintype.card 而不是 card。

text: The following is an example of calculating the cardinality of a finset, namely, the union of range n with a copy of range n that has been shifted by more than n . The calculation requires showing the the two sets in the union are disjoint; the first line of the proof yields the side condition Disjoint (range n) (image (fun i ↦ m + i) (range n)) , which is established at the end of the proof. The Disjoint predicate is too general to be directly useful to us, but the theorem disjoint_iff_ne puts it in a form we can use.
html: The following is an example of calculating the cardinality of a finset, namely, the union of range n with a copy of range n that has been shifted by more than n. The calculation requires showing the the two sets in the union are disjoint; the first line of the proof yields the side condition Disjoint (range n) (image (fun i ↦ m + i) (range n)), which is established at the end of the proof. The Disjoint predicate is too general to be directly useful to us, but the theorem disjoint_iff_ne puts it in a form we can use.
zh: 下面是一个计算有限集基数的例子：计算 range n 与其一个副本的并，其中这个副本被平移了超过 n 的距离。该计算要求证明并中的两个集合不交；证明的第一行产生旁条件 Disjoint (range n) (image (fun i ↦ m + i) (range n))，它在证明末尾得到证明。谓词 Disjoint 对我们来说过于一般，不能直接使用，但定理 disjoint_iff_ne 会把它化为可用的形式。

text: Throughout this section, omega will be a workhorse for us, for dealing with arithmetic calculations and inequalities.
html: Throughout this section, omega will be a workhorse for us, for dealing with arithmetic calculations and inequalities.
zh: 在本节中，omega 将是处理算术计算与不等式的主力工具。

text: Here is a more interesting example. Consider the subset of \(\{0, \ldots, n\} \times \{0, \ldots, n\}\) consisting of pairs \((i, j)\) such that \(i < j\) . If you think of these as lattice points in the coordinate plane, they constitute an upper triangle of the square with corners \((0, 0)\) and \((n, n)\) , not including the diagonal. The cardinality of the full square is \((n + 1)^2\) , and removing the size of the diagonal and halving the result shows us that the cardinality of the triangle is \(n (n + 1) / 2\) .
html: Here is a more interesting example. Consider the subset of \(\{0, \ldots, n\} \times \{0, \ldots, n\}\) consisting of pairs \((i, j)\) such that \(i < j\). If you think of these as lattice points in the coordinate plane, they constitute an upper triangle of the square with corners \((0, 0)\) and \((n, n)\), not including the diagonal. The cardinality of the full square is \((n + 1)^2\), and removing the size of the diagonal and halving the result shows us that the cardinality of the triangle is \(n (n + 1) / 2\).
zh: 下面是一个更有趣的例子。考虑 \(\{0, \ldots, n\} \times \{0, \ldots, n\}\) 中由满足 \(i < j\) 的点 \((i, j)\) 构成的子集。如果把它们看成坐标平面中的格点，它们构成以 \((0, 0)\) 和 \((n, n)\) 为顶点的正方形的上三角部分，但不包括对角线。整个正方形的基数为 \((n + 1)^2\)；减去对角线的大小后再除以二，可知该三角形的基数为 \(n (n + 1) / 2\)。

text: Alternatively, we note that the rows of the triangle have sizes \(0, 1, \ldots, n\) , so the cardinality is the sum of the first \(n\) positive integers. The first have of the proof below describes the triangle as the union of the rows, where row \(j\) consists of the numbers \(0, 1, ..., j - 1\) paired with \(j\) . In the proof below, the notation (., j) abbreviates the function fun i ↦ (i, j) . The rest of the proof is just a calculation with finset cardinalities.
html: Alternatively, we note that the rows of the triangle have sizes \(0, 1, \ldots, n\), so the cardinality is the sum of the first \(n\) positive integers. The first have of the proof below describes the triangle as the union of the rows, where row \(j\) consists of the numbers \(0, 1, ..., j - 1\) paired with \(j\). In the proof below, the notation (., j) abbreviates the function fun i ↦ (i, j). The rest of the proof is just a calculation with finset cardinalities.
zh: 另一种做法是注意该三角形各行的大小为 \(0, 1, \ldots, n\)，因此其基数就是前 \(n\) 个正整数之和。下面证明中的第一个 have 把三角形描述为各行之并，其中第 \(j\) 行由数 \(0, 1, ..., j - 1\) 与 \(j\) 配对而成。在下面的证明中，记号 (., j) 是函数 fun i ↦ (i, j) 的缩写。证明的其余部分只是关于有限集基数的计算。

text: The following variation on the proof does the calculation with fintypes instead of finsets. The type α ≃ β is the type of equivalences between α and β , consisting of a map in the forward direction, the map in the backward direction, and proofs that these two are inverse to one another. The first have in the proof shows that triangle n is equivalent to the disjoint union of Fin i as i ranges over Fin (n + 1) . Interestingly, the forward function and the reverse function are constructed with tactics, rather than written explicitly. Since they do nothing more than move data and information around, rfl establishes that they are inverses.
html: The following variation on the proof does the calculation with fintypes instead of finsets. The type α ≃ β is the type of equivalences between α and β, consisting of a map in the forward direction, the map in the backward direction, and proofs that these two are inverse to one another. The first have in the proof shows that triangle n is equivalent to the disjoint union of Fin i as i ranges over Fin (n + 1). Interestingly, the forward function and the reverse function are constructed with tactics, rather than written explicitly. Since they do nothing more than move data and information around, rfl establishes that they are inverses.
zh: 下面的证明变体使用有限类型而非有限集来完成计算。类型 α ≃ β 是 α 与 β 之间等价的类型，由正向映射、反向映射以及证明二者互为逆的证明组成。证明中的第一个 have 表明 triangle n 等价于 i 遍历 Fin (n + 1) 时各 Fin i 的不交并。有趣的是，正向函数和反向函数是用策略构造出来的，而不是显式写出的。由于它们只是搬运数据和信息，rfl 即可证明它们互为逆。

text: After that, rw [←Fintype.card_coe] rewrites #(triangle n) as the cardinality of the subtype { x // x ∈ triangle n } , and the rest of the proof is a calculation.
html: After that, rw [←Fintype.card_coe] rewrites #(triangle n) as the cardinality of the subtype { x // x ∈ triangle n }, and the rest of the proof is a calculation.
zh: 之后，rw [←Fintype.card_coe] 将 #(triangle n) 重写为子类型 { x // x ∈ triangle n } 的基数，证明的其余部分就是计算。

text: Here is yet another approach. The first line of the proof below reduces the problem to showing 2 * #(triangle n) = (n + 1) * n . We can do that by showing that two copies of the triangle exactly fill the rectangle range n ×ˢ range (n + 1) . As an exercise, see if you can fill in the steps of the calculation. In the solutions, we rely on omega extensively in the second-to-last step, but we unfortunately have to do a fair amount of work by hand.
html: Here is yet another approach. The first line of the proof below reduces the problem to showing 2 * #(triangle n) = (n + 1) * n. We can do that by showing that two copies of the triangle exactly fill the rectangle range n ×ˢ range (n + 1). As an exercise, see if you can fill in the steps of the calculation. In the solutions, we rely on omega extensively in the second-to-last step, but we unfortunately have to do a fair amount of work by hand.
zh: 还有另一种方法。下面证明的第一行把问题化归为证明 2 * #(triangle n) = (n + 1) * n。我们可以通过证明两个三角形的副本恰好填满矩形 range n ×ˢ range (n + 1) 来做到这一点。作为练习，请尝试补全该计算的步骤。在解答中，我们在倒数第二步大量依赖 omega，但遗憾的是仍须手工完成相当一部分工作。

text: You can convince yourself that we get the same triangle, shifted down, if we replace n by n + 1 and replace < by ≤ in the definition of triangle . The exercise below asks you to use this fact to show that the two triangles have the same size.
html: You can convince yourself that we get the same triangle, shifted down, if we replace n by n + 1 and replace < by ≤ in the definition of triangle. The exercise below asks you to use this fact to show that the two triangles have the same size.
zh: 你可以自行确信：若在 triangle 的定义中把 n 替换为 n + 1，并把 < 替换为 ≤，得到的只是向下平移后的同一个三角形。下面的练习要求你用这一事实证明两个三角形大小相同。

text: Let us close this section with an example and an exercise from a tutorial on combinatorics given by Bhavik Mehta at Lean for the Curious Mathematician in 2023. Suppose we have a bipartite graph with vertex sets s and t , such that for every a in s , there are at least three edges leaving a , and for every b in t , there is at most one edge entering b . Then the total number of edges in the graph is at least three times the cardinality of s and at most the cardinality of t , from which is follows that three times the cardinality of s is at most the cardinality of t . The following theorem implements this argument, where we use the relation r to represent the edges of the graph. The proof is an elegant calculation.
html: Let us close this section with an example and an exercise from a tutorial on combinatorics given by Bhavik Mehta at Lean for the Curious Mathematician in 2023. Suppose we have a bipartite graph with vertex sets s and t, such that for every a in s, there are at least three edges leaving a, and for every b in t, there is at most one edge entering b. Then the total number of edges in the graph is at least three times the cardinality of s and at most the cardinality of t, from which is follows that three times the cardinality of s is at most the cardinality of t. The following theorem implements this argument, where we use the relation r to represent the edges of the graph. The proof is an elegant calculation.
zh: 本节最后给出一个例子和一个练习，它们来自 Bhavik Mehta 于 2023 年在 Lean for the Curious Mathematician 中关于组合数学的tutorial。设有一个二分图，其顶点集为 s 和 t，并且对 s 中每个 a，至少有三条边从 a 发出；对 t 中每个 b，至多有一条边进入 b。于是图中的边总数至少是 s 的基数的三倍，且至多是 t 的基数，由此推出 s 的基数的三倍不超过 t 的基数。下面的定理实现了这个论证，其中我们用关系 r 表示图的边。该证明是一个优雅的计算。

text: The following exercise is also taken from Mehta’s tutorial. Suppose A is a subset of range (2 * n) with n + 1 elements. It’s easy to see that A must contain two consecutive integers, and hence two elements that are coprime. If you watch the tutorial, you will see that a good deal of effort was spent in establishing the following fact, which is now proved automatically by omega .
html: The following exercise is also taken from Mehta’s tutorial. Suppose A is a subset of range (2 * n) with n + 1 elements. It’s easy to see that A must contain two consecutive integers, and hence two elements that are coprime. If you watch the tutorial, you will see that a good deal of effort was spent in establishing the following fact, which is now proved automatically by omega.
zh: 下面的练习同样取自 Mehta 的教程。设 A 是 range (2 * n) 的一个子集，且有 n + 1 个元素。容易看出，A 必含两个连续整数，从而含有两个互素的元素。如果观看该教程，你会看到为了建立下面这个事实曾花费了大量精力，而如今它可由 omega 自动证明。

text: The solution to Mehta’s exercise uses the pigeonhole principle, in the form exists_lt_card_fiber_of_mul_lt_card_of_maps_to , to show that there are two distinct elements m and k in A such that m / 2 = k / 2 . See if you can complete the justification of that fact and then use it to finish the proof.
html: The solution to Mehta’s exercise uses the pigeonhole principle, in the form exists_lt_card_fiber_of_mul_lt_card_of_maps_to, to show that there are two distinct elements m and k in A such that m / 2 = k / 2. See if you can complete the justification of that fact and then use it to finish the proof.
zh: Mehta 练习的解法使用抽屉原理，其形式为 exists_lt_card_fiber_of_mul_lt_card_of_maps_to，来证明 A 中存在两个不同元素 m 和 k，使得 m / 2 = k / 2。请尝试补全这一事实的论证，然后用它完成证明。

text: Lean’s foundation allows us to define inductive types, that is, data types whose instances are generated from the bottom up. For example, the data type List α of lists of elements of α is generated by starting with the empty list, nil , and successively adding elements to the front the list. Below we will define a type of binary trees, BinTree , whose elements are generated by starting with the empty tree and building new trees by attaching a new node to two existing trees.
html: Lean’s foundation allows us to define inductive types, that is, data types whose instances are generated from the bottom up. For example, the data type List α of lists of elements of α is generated by starting with the empty list, nil, and successively adding elements to the front the list. Below we will define a type of binary trees, BinTree, whose elements are generated by starting with the empty tree and building new trees by attaching a new node to two existing trees.
zh: Lean 的基础允许我们定义归纳类型，即其实例自底向上生成的数据类型。例如，由 α 的元素组成的列表数据类型 List α，是从空列表 nil 开始，并不断向列表前端添加元素而生成的。下面我们将定义一种二叉树类型 BinTree，其元素从空树开始生成，并通过把一个新节点连接到两棵已有树上来构造新的树。

text: In Lean, one can define inductive types whose objects are infinite, like countably branching well-founded trees. Finite inductive definitions are commonly used in discrete mathematics, however, especially in those branches of discrete mathematics that are relevant to computer science. Lean provides not only the means to define such types, but also principles of induction and definition by recursion. For example, the data type List α is defined inductively:
html: In Lean, one can define inductive types whose objects are infinite, like countably branching well-founded trees. Finite inductive definitions are commonly used in discrete mathematics, however, especially in those branches of discrete mathematics that are relevant to computer science. Lean provides not only the means to define such types, but also principles of induction and definition by recursion. For example, the data type List α is defined inductively:
zh: 在 Lean 中，可以定义对象为无限的归纳类型，例如可数分支的良基树。不过，有限归纳定义在离散数学中很常用，尤其是在与计算机科学相关的离散数学分支中。Lean 不仅提供定义这类类型的方法，也提供归纳原则和递归定义原则。例如，数据类型 List α 是归纳定义的：

text: The inductive definition says that every element of List α is either nil , the empty list, or cons a as , where a is an element of α and as is a list of elements of α . The constructors are properly named List.nil and List.cons , but you can use the shorter notation when the List namespace is open. When the List namespace is not open, you can write .nil and .cons a as anywhere that Lean expects a list, and Lean will automatically insert the List qualifier. Throughout this section, we will put temporary definitions in separate namespaces like MyListSpace to avoid conflicts with the standard library. Outside the temporary namespace, we revert to using the standard library definitions.
html: The inductive definition says that every element of List α is either nil, the empty list, or cons a as, where a is an element of α and as is a list of elements of α. The constructors are properly named List.nil and List.cons, but you can use the shorter notation when the List namespace is open. When the List namespace is not open, you can write .nil and .cons a as anywhere that Lean expects a list, and Lean will automatically insert the List qualifier. Throughout this section, we will put temporary definitions in separate namespaces like MyListSpace to avoid conflicts with the standard library. Outside the temporary namespace, we revert to using the standard library definitions.
zh: 该归纳定义说明，List α 的每个元素要么是空列表 nil，要么是 cons a as，其中 a 是 α 的一个元素，而 as 是由 α 的元素组成的列表。这两个构造子严格说名为 List.nil 和 List.cons，但当 List 命名空间打开时，可以使用较短的记号。当 List 命名空间未打开时，只要 Lean 期待一个列表，就可以写 .nil 和 .cons a as，Lean 会自动插入 List 限定。在本节中，我们会把临时定义放在诸如 MyListSpace 这样的独立命名空间中，以避免与标准库冲突。在临时命名空间之外，我们则恢复使用标准库定义。

text: Lean defines the notation [] for nil and :: for cons , and you can write [a, b, c] for a :: b :: c :: [] . The append and map functions are defined recursively as follows:
html: Lean defines the notation [] for nil and :: for cons, and you can write [a, b, c] for a :: b :: c :: []. The append and map functions are defined recursively as follows:
zh: Lean 将记号 [] 定义为 nil，将 :: 定义为 cons；并且可以把 [a, b, c] 写成 a :: b :: c :: []。函数 append 和 map 按如下方式递归定义：

text: Notice that there is a base case and a recursive case. In each case, the two defining clauses hold definitionally:
html: Notice that there is a base case and a recursive case. In each case, the two defining clauses hold definitionally:
zh: 注意这里有一个基本情形和一个递归情形。在每种情形中，这两个定义子句都按定义成立：

text: The functions append and map are defined in the standard library, and append as bs can be written as as ++ bs .
html: The functions append and map are defined in the standard library, and append as bs can be written as as ++ bs.
zh: 函数 append 和 map 已在标准库中定义，而 append as bs 可以写成 as ++ bs。

text: Lean allows you to write proofs by induction following the structure of the definition.
html: Lean allows you to write proofs by induction following the structure of the definition.
zh: Lean 允许你按照定义的结构写出归纳证明。

text: You can also use the induction' tactic.
html: You can also use the induction' tactic.
zh: 也可以使用策略 induction'。

text: Of course, these theorems are already in the standard library. As an exercise, try defining a function reverse in the MyListSpace3 namespace (to avoid conflicting with the standard List.reverse ) that reverses a list. You can use #eval reverse [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] to test it out. The most straightforward definition of reverse requires quadratic time, but don’t worry about that. You can jump to the definition of List.reverse in the standard library to see a linear-time implementation. Try proving reverse (as ++ bs) = reverse bs ++ reverse as and reverse (reverse as) = as . You can use cons_append and append_assoc , but you may need to come up with auxiliary lemmas and prove them.
html: Of course, these theorems are already in the standard library. As an exercise, try defining a function reverse in the MyListSpace3 namespace (to avoid conflicting with the standard List.reverse) that reverses a list. You can use #eval reverse [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] to test it out. The most straightforward definition of reverse requires quadratic time, but don’t worry about that. You can jump to the definition of List.reverse in the standard library to see a linear-time implementation. Try proving reverse (as ++ bs) = reverse bs ++ reverse as and reverse (reverse as) = as. You can use cons_append and append_assoc, but you may need to come up with auxiliary lemmas and prove them.
zh: 当然，这些定理已经在标准库中。作为练习，请尝试在命名空间 MyListSpace3 中定义一个函数 reverse（以避免与标准的 List.reverse 冲突），用于反转列表。可以用 #eval reverse [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] 测试它。最直接的 reverse 定义需要二次时间，但不必为此担心。可以跳转到标准库中 List.reverse 的定义，查看一个线性时间实现。请尝试证明 reverse (as ++ bs) = reverse bs ++ reverse as 和 reverse (reverse as) = as。可以使用 cons_append 和 append_assoc，但你可能需要提出并证明若干辅助引理。

text: For another example, consider the following inductive definition of binary trees together with functions to compute the size and depth of a binary tree.
html: For another example, consider the following inductive definition of binary trees together with functions to compute the size and depth of a binary tree.
zh: 作为另一个例子，考虑下面的二叉树归纳定义，以及用于计算二叉树大小和深度的函数。

text: It is convenient to count the empty binary tree as a binary tree of size 0 and depth 0. In the literature, this data type is sometimes called the extended binary trees . Including the empty tree means, for example, that we can define the tree node empty (node empty empty) consisting of a root node, an empty left subtree, and a right subtree consisting of a single node.
html: It is convenient to count the empty binary tree as a binary tree of size 0 and depth 0. In the literature, this data type is sometimes called the extended binary trees. Including the empty tree means, for example, that we can define the tree node empty (node empty empty) consisting of a root node, an empty left subtree, and a right subtree consisting of a single node.
zh: 把空二叉树计为大小为 0、深度为 0 的二叉树是方便的。在文献中，这种数据类型有时称为extended binary trees。包含空树意味着，例如我们可以定义树 node empty (node empty empty)，它由一个根节点、一棵空左子树和一棵只含单个节点的右子树组成。

text: Here is an important inequality relating the size and the depth:
html: Here is an important inequality relating the size and the depth:
zh: 下面是联系大小与深度的一个重要不等式：

text: Try proving the following inequality, which is somewhat easier. Remember, if you do a proof by induction as in the previous theorem, you have to delete the := by .
html: Try proving the following inequality, which is somewhat easier. Remember, if you do a proof by induction as in the previous theorem, you have to delete the := by.
zh: 请尝试证明下面这个不等式，它稍微容易一些。记住，如果像前一个定理那样用归纳法证明，必须删除 := by。

text: Also define the flip operation on binary trees, which recursively swaps the left and right subtrees.
html: Also define the flip operation on binary trees, which recursively swaps the left and right subtrees.
zh: 还请定义二叉树上的 flip 操作，它递归地交换左子树与右子树。

text: If you did it right, the proof of the following should be rfl .
html: If you did it right, the proof of the following should be rfl.
zh: 如果定义正确，下面命题的证明应当是 <cite>rfl</cite>。

text: Prove the following:
html: Prove the following:
zh: 证明如下命题：

text: We close this section with some formal logic. The following is an inductive definition of propositional formulas.
html: We close this section with some formal logic. The following is an inductive definition of propositional formulas.
zh: 本节最后讨论一些形式逻辑。下面给出命题公式的归纳定义。

text: Every propositional formula is either a variable var n , the constant false fls , or a compound formula of the form conj A B , disj A B , or impl A B . With ordinary mathematical notation, these are commonly written \(p_n\) , \(\bot\) , \(A \wedge B\) , \(A \vee B\) , and \(A \to B\) , respectively. The other propositional connectives can be defined in terms of these; for example, we can define \(\neg A\) as \(A \to \bot\) and \(A \leftrightarrow B\) as \((A \to B) \wedge (B \to A)\) .
html: Every propositional formula is either a variable var n, the constant false fls, or a compound formula of the form conj A B, disj A B, or impl A B. With ordinary mathematical notation, these are commonly written \(p_n\), \(\bot\), \(A \wedge B\), \(A \vee B\), and \(A \to B\), respectively. The other propositional connectives can be defined in terms of these; for example, we can define \(\neg A\) as \(A \to \bot\) and \(A \leftrightarrow B\) as \((A \to B) \wedge (B \to A)\).
zh: 每个命题公式要么是变量 var n，要么是假常量 fls，要么是形如 conj A B、disj A B 或 impl A B 的复合公式。用通常的数学记号，它们分别常写为 \(p_n\)、\(\bot\)、\(A \wedge B\)、\(A \vee B\) 和 \(A \to B\)。其他命题联结词可以由这些联结词定义；例如，可以把 \(\neg A\) 定义为 \(A \to \bot\)，把 \(A \leftrightarrow B\) 定义为 \((A \to B) \wedge (B \to A)\)。

text: Having defined the data type of propositional formulas, we define what it means to evaluate a propositional formula with respect to an assignment v of Boolean truth values to the variables.
html: Having defined the data type of propositional formulas, we define what it means to evaluate a propositional formula with respect to an assignment v of Boolean truth values to the variables.
zh: 定义了命题公式这一数据类型之后，我们定义在把布尔真值赋给变量的赋值 v 下，如何求一个命题公式的值。

text: The next definition specifies the set of variables occurring in a formula, and the subsequent theorem shows that evaluating a formula on two truth assignments that agree on its variables yields the same value.
html: The next definition specifies the set of variables occurring in a formula, and the subsequent theorem shows that evaluating a formula on two truth assignments that agree on its variables yields the same value.
zh: 下一个定义给出公式中出现的变量集合，随后定理表明：若两个真值赋值在某公式的变量上相同，则在这两个赋值下计算该公式会得到相同的值。

text: Noticing the repetition, we can be clever about the use of automation.
html: Noticing the repetition, we can be clever about the use of automation.
zh: 注意到这些重复，我们可以更聪明地使用自动化。

text: The function subst A m C describes the result of substituting the formula C for every occurrence of the variable var m in the formula A .
html: The function subst A m C describes the result of substituting the formula C for every occurrence of the variable var m in the formula A.
zh: 函数 subst A m C 描述把公式 C 代入公式 A 中变量 var m 每一次出现处所得的结果。

text: As an example, show that substituting for a variable that does not occur in a formula has no effect:
html: As an example, show that substituting for a variable that does not occur in a formula has no effect:
zh: 作为例子，证明：若一个变量不出现在公式中，则对该变量作代入没有任何影响：

text: The following theorem says something more subtle and interesting: evaluating A.subst n C on a truth assignment v is the same as evaluating A on a truth assignment that assigns the value of C to var n . See if you can prove it.
html: The following theorem says something more subtle and interesting: evaluating A.subst n C on a truth assignment v is the same as evaluating A on a truth assignment that assigns the value of C to var n. See if you can prove it.
zh: 下面的定理说的是更细致也更有趣的事实：在真值赋值 v 下求 A.subst n C 的值，等同于在一个把 var n 赋为 C 的值的真值赋值下求 A 的值。请尝试证明它。
